name: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana
description: A national public health insurance fund of the Government of India that aims to provide free access to health insurance coverage for low income earners in the country.
eligibility: Low-income families identified in the SECC 2011 database. Lack of housing, destitutes, manual scavenger families, primitive tribal groups.
benefits: Health insurance cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization.

name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN)
description: An initiative by the government of India in which all farmers will get up to ₹6,000 per year as minimum income support.
eligibility: Small and marginal farmers holding cultivable land up to 2 hectares.
benefits: ₹6,000 per year transferred in three equal installments of ₹2,000 directly into the bank accounts.

name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY)
description: An initiative by Government of India in which affordable housing will be provided to the urban poor with a target of building 20 million affordable houses.
eligibility: Economically Weaker Section (EWS), Low Income Group (LIG), and Middle Income Group (MIG). Must not own a pucca house.
benefits: Subsidy on home loan interest rates up to 6.5%.

name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
description: A savings scheme targeted at the parents of girl children, encouraging them to build a fund for the future education and marriage of their female child.
eligibility: Parents or legal guardians of a girl child. The child must be 10 years or younger at the time of account opening.
benefits: High interest rate, tax benefits under Section 80C, maturity on completion of 21 years from account opening.

name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY)
description: A scheme launched by the Hon'ble Prime Minister to provide loans up to 10 lakh to the non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen who has a business plan for a non-farm sector income generating activity such as manufacturing, processing, trading or service sector and whose credit need is less than ₹10 lakh.
benefits: Loans classified as Shishu (up to 50k), Kishore (50k to 5L), and Tarun (5L to 10L) with competitive interest rates.

name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship Scheme (NMMSS)
description: A centrally sponsored scheme to award scholarships to meritorious students of economically weaker sections to arrest their drop out at class VIII.
eligibility: Students studying in class IX in Government, Local Body and Government aided schools. Parental income from all sources should not exceed ₹3,50,000/- per annum.
benefits: Scholarship of ₹12,000/- per annum (₹1000/- per month) from class IX to class XII.

name: Atal Pension Yojana (APY)
description: A pension scheme for citizens of India, primarily focused on the unorganized sector workers.
eligibility: All citizens of India between 18-40 years of age having a savings bank account/post office savings bank account.
benefits: Guaranteed minimum monthly pension between ₹1,000 and ₹5,000 depending on the contribution after the age of 60 years.

name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
description: An Indian labour law and social security measure that aims to guarantee the 'right to work'.
eligibility: Adult members of any rural household willing to do public work-related unskilled manual work.
benefits: At least 100 days of guaranteed wage employment in a financial year to every rural household.

name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
description: A one-year life insurance scheme renewable from year to year offering coverage for death due to any reason.
eligibility: Available to people in the age group of 18 to 50 years having a bank account who give their consent to join / enable auto-debit.
benefits: Life cover of Rs. 2 Lakh in case of death for any reason against a premium of Rs. 436/- per annum.

name: PM Vishwakarma Scheme
description: A central sector scheme to support traditional artisans and craftspeople of rural and urban India.
eligibility: An artisan or craftsperson working with hands and tools and engaged in one of the 18 family-based traditional trades. Minimum age 18 years.
benefits: PM Vishwakarma Certificate & ID card, collateral-free credit support up to ₹3 lakh, stipend of ₹500/day for skill training, toolkit incentive of ₹15,000.
